UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT SOJOURN PROPERTIES INC., AND STEVEN KING'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT; THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING STANDS AS FOLLOWS; PROSECUTION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED ITS BURDEN TO SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT ARISES OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY, I.E. FILING AND MAINTAINING UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION CUD-11-639673. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO FILE TIMELY OPPOSITION ON NOVEMBER 14, 2013. INSTEAD, PLAINTIFF FILED AND SERVED BY MAIL FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ON NOVEMBER 15, 2013. PLAINTIFF'S PIECEMEAL OPPOSITION FILED ON DECEMBER 16, 19, 23, AND 27 2013 IS STRICKEN AS UNTIMELY. EVEN IF OPPOSITION WAS CONSIDERED, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN UNDER THE SECOND PRONG OF CCP 425.16 AND SHOW A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS ON THE COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF MAY NOT AMEND THE COMPLAINT BEFORE THE HEARING ON ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. SALMA V. CAPON (2008) 161 CAL.APP.4TH 1275.PLAITFF' RELIANCE ON LAM V. NGO (2001) 91 CAL.APP.4TH 832 FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS THE OPERATIVE PLEADING UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES IS MISPLACED. IN LAM, FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT WAS FILED BEFORE ANTI-SLAPP AND THE ISSUE WAS TIMELINESS OF THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Hanlin of Hanlin Law telephone number 415-818-4241 for Plaintiff Yoshabel Clements.
Peter Weber of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney telephone number 415-788-1900 for Defendant Brillant.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS BRILLANT LAW FIRM, DAVID BRILLANT AND BRIAN CARIDEO'S anti-SLAPP motion to strike is granted. Notwithstanding that plaintiff Yoshabel Clements submitted opposing papers after the deadline to do so, the court considered all of the opposing papers. The parties agree that a claim for malicious prosecution satisifies the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis because it is based on protected petitioning activity. (Jarrow Formulas, Inc. v. LaMarche (2003) 31 Cal.4th 728.) The elements of a malicious prosecution cause of action are (1) favorable termination on the merits; (2) lack of probable cause; and (3) malice. (Sheldon Appel Co. v. Albert & Oliker (1989) 47 Cal.3d 863, 871.) Ms. Clements fails to present evidence showing either a lack of probable cause or malice. Probable cause exists "if any reasonable attorney would have thought the claim tenable." (Id. at 886.) The deficiencies in the 3-day notice to cure or quit and the uncertainty whether the parties expressly agreed as part of the lease that plaintiff would submit a rental application do not show that the claim was legally untenable. In a decision that is law of the case, the Court of Appeal already concluded that Ms. Clements could not show a lack of probable cause when it affirmed the granting of the landlord's anti-SLAPP motion to strike. Plaintiff has not highlighted any additional information that shows that this court should depart from either the conclusion or reasoning of the Court of Appeal. Ms. Clements also fails to present sufficient evidence of malice. A lack of probable cause, by itself, does not show malice. The evidence that plaintiff presents is similar to the evidence that the plaintiff cited in Daniels v. Robbins (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 204, 227, where the court found insufficient evidence to infer malice. Order signed in open Court. The prevailing defendants are entitled to fees and costs, but must seek them through another motion (language included in the order).

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-30-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Attorney Michael Beuselinck (415) 981-6630 for Sojourn Properties Inc., and Steven King the moving party/ Attorney John Hanlin for Yoshabel Clements the opposing party.

After hearing arguments from both parties in the matter of DEFENDANT SOJOURN PROPERTIES INC., and STEVEN KING's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT; the Court's tentative Ruling Stands as follows;
Prosecution is GRANTED. Defendant shifted its burden to show that Plaintiff's complaint arises out of protected activity, i.e. filing and maintaining unlawful detainer action CUD-11-639673. Plaintiff failed to file timely opposition on November 14, 2013. Instead, Plaintiff filed and served by mail First Amended Complaint on November 15, 2013. Plaintiff's piecemeal opposition filed on December 16, 19, 23, and 27 2013 is stricken as untimely. Even if opposition was considered, Plaintiff fails to sustain its burden under the second prong of CCP 425.16 and show a probability of prevailing on the merits on the Complaint. Plaintiff may not amend the complaint before the hearing on anti-SLAPP motion. Salma v. Capon (2008) 161 Cal.App.4th 1275.Plaitff' reliance on Lam v. Ngo (2001) 91 Cal.App.4th 832 for the proposition that First Amended Complaint is the operative pleading under the circumstances is misplaced. In Lam, First Amended Complaint was filed before anti-SLAPP and the issue was timeliness of the anti-SLAPP motion. Attorneys' fees and costs granted in the amount of $3,400 within 14 days of this order. The Court takes Judicial Notice of Transcripts from depositions of Steven King (2 volumes) and Galo Dizb (2 volumes). Order signed and filed in open court. Judge Harry M. Dorfman, Clerk J. Rumsey, Reporter Ramona Reinhardt, #2677, (510)652-4337. (501)
